metadata.greeting: This weekend looks fantastic for getting outdoors! There are lots of free activities at the museums, and plenty to see in the parks. I've also spotted some lovely events coming up in the next few weeks that you can book ahead for.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: A museum of creativity for children and young teenagers, with interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: The sensory Play Gallery is perfect for a 3-year-old, and there's space to run around.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 or 48 to Bethnal Green station, then walk. Or Overground to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check for special drop-in activities on the day.

activities.title: Play Saturdays
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Drop-in creative play sessions for families.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on and engaging, with different activities each week.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 or 48 to Bethnal Green station, then walk. Or Overground to Bethnal Green.

activities.title: Family Activity: Micro to Macro!
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: A drop-in family activity exploring the world from the tiny to the enormous.
activities.whyItWorks: Encourages curiosity and creativity.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 26, 55 or 48 to Bethnal Green station, then walk. Or Overground to Bethnal Green.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A small city farm with animals, gardens and a cafe.
activities.whyItWorks: Toddlers love seeing the animals, and it's a great way to learn about where food comes from.

transport.fromHackney: Walk, cycle, or bus.
transport.tip: Check for special events on their website.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A small playground designed for toddlers with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Safe and fun for toddlers, with a sweet pond theme.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 30, 388 to Stratford station, then walk through the park.

activities.title: Victoria Park
activities.description: East London's most famous park, with playgrounds and green space.
activities.whyItWorks: Plenty of space to run around, plus playgrounds for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Bus 277 to Victoria Park.
transport.tip: Check the Victoria Park Facebook page for details of Easter activities.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: A hidden gem, located in an area of London called Hoxton. There are lots of hands-on things for them to do, even an old computer with basic games like the original Sims and pinball.
activities.whyItWorks: There are lots of hands-on things for them to do, even an old computer with basic games.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 243 to stop Hoxton Station
transport.tip: The Museum of the Home also has a beautiful little, enclosed garden, so it's a perfect museum to visit in spring or summer.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: There's two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground. The museum hosts Baby Storytelling plus crafts and singalong time for babies and toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: There's two floors of immersive play space known as the Story Worlds as well as the Story Garden playground.

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 276 to stop Angel Lane
transport.tip: There are free activity sheets specially designed for different ages, and so many hands-on activities for them to enjoy as they explore.

activities.title: National Maritime Museum
activities.description: Maritime museum with hands-on exhibits and a children's gallery.
activities.whyItWorks: The All Hands Children's Gallery is specifically designed just for kids, so make sure to check it out. On Saturdays, they have “Character Encounters”, which will really bring your visit to life.

transport.fromHackney: DLR to Greenwich.
transport.tip: Greenwich Park is nearby for a run around.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 5BD
activities.description: Iconic museum with dinosaur skeletons and interactive exhibits.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots to see and do for all ages, including a dedicated children's area.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Can get very busy, so go early.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2DD
activities.description: Interactive exhibits for kids to discover how the world works. And, if you have a budding young astronaut, they'll adore the gallery dedicated to space exploration, featuring a full-sized model of the lander that put the first men on the moon!
activities.whyItWorks: If you're happy to spend a little, it offers a great hands-on experience for the kids with experiment demonstrations, talks, activities and educational play zones.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small zoo in the Hampstead Heath area of London – it's completely free to enter, and it's home to rare species of birds and animals. They've got wallabies, lemurs, Scottish wildcat and more!
activities.whyItWorks: Also, in the same area, there are swimming ponds and a lido too so it could be worth a visit if you want to make a day of it this summer – there's a lot of family-friendly things to do in this part of London.

transport.fromHackney: Take the overground to Hampstead Heath station, then walk

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: Most of the action takes place around the enormous Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, but there's also a sensory trail, teepees and plenty of seating for parents to rest their weary legs!
activities.whyItWorks: A popular park is the Princess of Wales' Memorial Playground – most of the action takes place around the enormous Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, but there's also a sensory trail, teepees and plenty of seating for parents to rest their weary legs!

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Notting Hill Gate Station

activities.title: Sky Garden
activities.description: London's highest public garden, with 360° views of the city.
activities.whyItWorks: Spectacular views and a breath of fresh air, absolutely free.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Monument station.
transport.tip: Must book in advance - spaces are limited.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: Battersea Park offers a scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Families can enjoy informal Easter egg hunts, picnics, or just a relaxing walk through this inner-city green space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction
transport.tip: The pagoda and lakeside paths make it a picturesque destination for photography and leisurely strolls.

activities.title: SEND Play
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2RL
activities.description: Drop-in sessions designed for children with sensory differences or SEND.
activities.whyItWorks: Welcoming and inclusive environment.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Play After School
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2RL
activities.description: Drop-in play sessions after school hours.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun and creative activities.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Sound Explorers
activities.address: Cromwell Road, South Kensington, London SW7 2RL
activities.description: Drop-in music and sound exploration sessions.
activities.whyItWorks: A sensory experience for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Tube: Overground to Highbury & Islington, then Victoria line to South Kensington.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Easter Egg trail, indoor and outdoor play, and animal encounters.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of activities to keep a 3-year-old entertained for hours.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Hounslow West, then bus H32.

activities.title: Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt
activities.description: Hunt for Lindt Gold Bunny statues in the palace gardens.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun for the whole family, with a chocolate reward at the end.

transport.fromHackney: Train to Hampton Court from Waterloo.

activities.title: Zoo-normous Egg Hunt
activities.description: Wander past giraffes, lions, monkeys and more incredible wildlife as you search for hidden eggs on a special Easter egg hunt (included in zoo entry).
activities.whyItWorks: A great way to combine animal spotting with an Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Tube to Regent's Park or Camden Town.

activities.title: The Lion Inside
activities.description: A fantastic performance at Unicorn Theatre
activities.whyItWorks: A great show for children ages 3 to 7 years old

transport.fromHackney: Take the Jubilee Line to London Bridge Station

activities.title: Meet the Designer
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Meet the Designer event at Young V&A
activities.whyItWorks: Open studio

transport.fromHackney: Take the bus 26 to stop Bethnal Green Station
